engine.ladder_reasoning: A film composer whose demo reel and Netflix-title credits (Clara, Marmaduke) ARE the proof; visitor is browsing the work/sound, not evaluating a product or vetting credentials. Portfolio-is-proof beats trust.

goal.one_action: get the visitor to license/stream his music or email for custom-music inquiries

structure.archetype_note: Bare WordPress.com site; homepage is an empty shell, substance lives on about + demo-reel pages; most nav items (license/stream/production) are external Linktree/Spotify redirects rather than on-site pages.

proposed_new.name: media-hub
proposed_new.evidence: Primary nav routes to owned external music properties — 'license music'/'stream music' (Linktree) and 'music production for artists' (Spotify playlist), plus YouTube channel — a standing discography/content library living off-site, not an on-page section.

design_style.none_fits_because: Default WordPress.com chrome, no deliberate visual design (empty fonts/colors, text-heavy, WP.com sign-up/log-in shell); no craft or authority styling to classify.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is 'rendered' not 'full'; the core demo-reel video is password-protected and did not render, so the primary proof asset could not be inspected. Structure and intent are otherwise clear.

gap_findings: Site's spine is a set of OUTBOUND redirects to owned external music channels (Linktree licensing/streaming, Spotify, YouTube) — closest is the proposed media-hub, but that concept was coined for owned podcast/episode libraries; here it's a link-router to external distribution/licensing, which strains the definition.

The demo-reel 'proof' is password-gated video for named Netflix titles — a credentialing-via-credits pattern (worked-on famous titles) that neither results nor testimonials cleanly captures for a creative-services creator.
